lesson: Levels that lack early interaction (enemies, hazards, or meaningful choices) cause player disengagement and low ratings even when the layout is otherwise competent and completable.
conditions: no enemies or hazards in opening segment
no branching paths or meaningful choices early
linear traversal dominates first 10+ seconds
competent platforming layout present
no enemies present
no environmental hazards
linear platforming only
spawn area has no enemies, hazards, or path choices for 10+ seconds
linear traversal dominates early level
outcome: player completes level but rates experience as boring/too easy and disengages before gameplay meaningfully begins

merged_from.lesson: A level that opens with a long safe corridor and no immediate interaction (enemies, hazards, or branching) causes players to disengage before gameplay begins.

lesson: When a level contains multiple visually similar vertical connections (ladders, shafts, or gaps) without clear signposting or spatial differentiation, players cannot identify the critical path, leading to extensive backtracking, wrong-route attempts, and eventual abandonment.
conditions: multi-tier or vertical layout
multiple visually similar vertical connections (ladders, shafts, gaps)
no minimap, signposting, or visual differentiation of the goal path
objective located behind or above one specific connection
vertical corridor with 3+ ladders in close horizontal proximity
critical path requires specific ladder sequence
multi-tier layout
sparse vertical connections
no minimap or signposting
multiple ladder shafts in a horizontal corridor
no visual or spatial differentiation of the goal path
exit/boss located at corridor end behind vertical gate
no visual differentiation (color, markers, lighting) between ladders
outcome: player wanders horizontally and vertically, repeatedly attempts wrong routes, stalls at dead ends, and quits without reaching the objective

merged_from.lesson: When a level's critical path requires ascending through a specific ladder shaft among many visually similar ones, players cannot distinguish the correct route without explicit signposting.
Multiple visually similar ladders in a vertical corridor without signposting cause players to repeatedly ascend the wrong shaft, fall, and re-climb, wasting time and increasing perceived difficulty.

lesson: Placing ranged enemies on elevated platforms above narrow horizontal corridors creates unavoidable damage zones that feel unfair rather than challenging.
conditions: ranged enemies on high platforms
narrow corridor below
limited dodge space
outcome: repeated combat deaths in same corridor, frustration

lesson: Deep pits at the bottom of a level without any climb-out mechanic (ladders, ledges, or wall-jump surfaces) act as permanent fail states that trap players and force a restart.
conditions: pit depth exceeds maximum jump height
no vertical escape routes inside the pit
pit located on critical path or near exit
outcome: player falls in, cannot escape, loops at bottom until quitting or timing out

lesson: When a level opens with a dense cluster of enemies right at the spawn corridor, players who lack offensive upgrades or clear dodge space take repeated unavoidable damage before they can even orient themselves.
conditions: multiple enemies placed in the first horizontal corridor
narrow corridor limits dodge movement
player starts with minimal combat tools
outcome: early deaths, frustration, long stall at spawn before first meaningful movement

lesson: Always validate that the spawn tile has at least one orthogonally adjacent empty tile before finalizing a level; a single solid-tile enclosure turns the entire playtest into a hard lock.
conditions: procedural or hand-authored level generation
spawn marker placed without runtime connectivity check
outcome: player is trapped immediately, cannot move, rates experience 1/5 and quits

lesson: Vertical level layouts with overlapping sightlines between tiers allow players to trivialize combat by attacking enemies from safe elevations outside intended engagement zones.
conditions: multi-tier vertical layout
ladders or platforms connecting tiers
enemies placed on platforms with clear vertical sightlines to other tiers
no line-of-sight blockers or engagement range limits
outcome: player kills enemies from different level tiers without risk, bypassing intended combat challenge

lesson: Repetitive staircase traversal without vertical variation (drops, shafts, multi-tier platforms) creates a flat difficulty curve that feels like a hallway, not a vertical level.
conditions: primary path is a uniform ascending stair pattern
no vertical branches, pits, or layered platforms
enemies placed on same plane as traversal
outcome: player completes level quickly with low challenge and explicitly criticizes lack of vertical diversity

lesson: Extended horizontal corridors with sparse verticality and minimal enemy pressure create a 'hallway' feel that players complete but rate as trivial, even with a high fun score, because the challenge never escalates.
conditions: long horizontal spans >2000px
enemy density <0.01 per tile
vertical variation limited to isolated platforms
completion time >240s with <5 deaths
outcome: player completes quickly, backtracks extensively, rates experience as too easy despite length

lesson: A boss that does not actively seek, attack, or react to the player at a meaningful distance turns the level's climax into a non-event, negating all prior traversal effort.
conditions: boss entity present at level end
boss has no aggro range or pursuit behavior
player can approach boss without triggering engagement
flat or open arena allows safe approach
outcome: player reaches boss, waits or attacks freely, defeats it without risk, rates experience as incomplete or broken

lesson: Enemy clusters at ladder exits or corridor chokepoints where the player must commit to a climb or jump create unavoidable damage zones that feel unfair rather than challenging.
conditions: enemies positioned at ladder tops or narrow corridor entries
player has no invincibility frames or dodge tools during climb/jump commitment
no safe observation point before engagement
enemies positioned at ladder exits or narrow corridor entries
outcome: repeated combat deaths at same locations, high hits taken, player stalls or quits

merged_from.lesson: Enemy clusters placed at ladder tops or corridor chokepoints where the player must commit to a jump or climb create unavoidable damage zones that feel unfair, not challenging.

lesson: In tall, fortress-style levels with extended vertical shafts, ground-only enemies fail to contest the player's climb, creating a difficulty vacuum that flying or projectile enemies would fill.
conditions: vertical level with long ladder shafts or climbable structures
enemies limited to ground-based types
player has ranged or upward attack capability
outcome: player ascends unchallenged, combat feels trivial despite imposing architecture
